title: Method and device for detecting middle ear content and/or condition
abstract: Some embodiments relate to a device for assessing middle ear content or condition, comprising: a body which tapers towards a narrow end having a diameter small enough to be introduced into an external ear canal; a plurality of light sources arranged peripherally around the wide end of the body and directed such that the emitted light is transmitted through a solid portion of the body; a distally facing imager for obtaining images of the tympanic membrane; the device being in communication with circuitry configured to analyze spectral images obtained by the imager to assess middle ear content or condition.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A device for assessing middle ear content or condition, comprising: a body which tapers towards a narrow end having a diameter small enough to be introduced into an external ear canal; a plurality of light sources arranged peripherally around said wide end of said body and directed such that the emitted light is transmitted through a solid portion of said body; a distally facing imager for obtaining images of the tympanic membrane; said device being in communication with circuitry configured to analyze spectral images obtained by said imager to assess middle ear content or condition. 2. The device according to claim 1, wherein said plurality of light sources are configured to emit light independently and sequentially; each of said light sources emitting light at a single wavelength. 3. The device according to claim 1, wherein said imager is equipped with per pixel wide bandpass filters. 4. The device according to claim 1, wherein said imager occupies a volume within said cylindrical extension such that light emitted by said plurality of light sources travels around said imager in a ring shaped pattern. 5. The device according to claim 1, wherein said circuitry is configured to generate a 2D image of the tympanic membrane using said spectral images. 6. The device according to claim 5, wherein said circuitry is programmed to detect fluoroscopic fingerprints of pathogens in said 2D image. 7. The device according to claim 1, wherein said imager comprises a CMOS sensor matrix. 8. The device according to claim 1, wherein said plurality of light sources comprise between 4-16 LEDs peripherally arranged around said wide end of said cone shaped body, each LED emitting light at a single wavelength selected from the range of 200-1000 nm. 9. The device according to claim 8, wherein said circuitry comprises a processor which is pre-programmed with at least one known fluorescence fingerprint of a pathogen and is configured to compare said spectral image acquired by said imager to said known fluorescence fingerprint. 10. The device according to claim 9, wherein said wide bandpass filters are RGB filters and wherein said fluorescence fingerprint is defined by a set of wavelengths that are within wavelength ranges detectable by said RGB filters. 11. The device according to claim 1, comprising 4 light sources, each positioned to illuminate a quadrant area of the tympanic membrane. 12. The device according to claim 11, wherein simultaneous activation of all 4 light sources produces a uniform illumination of the tympanic membrane. 13. The device according to claim 1, wherein said solid portion of said cone shaped body is formed of a transparent material. 14. The device according to claim 1, wherein said circuitry is configured for transferring data to one or more of: a clinician, a database, cloud storage, external memory, remote server. 15. The device according to claim 1, comprising a handle extending proximally from said wide end of said cone shaped body. 16. The device according to claim 1, wherein said circuitry is in communication with a screen display. 17. The device according to claim 1, wherein said cylindrical extension further comprises a set of radio frequency coils and circuity configured for measuring a magnetic field detected in response to passing of electrical current through said coils, said magnetic field indicative of presence of fluid behind the tympanic membrane. 18. A method for assessing middle ear content or condition, comprising: emitting light from a plurality of light sources sequentially towards the tympanic membrane; detecting the light arriving from the tympanic membrane via an imager equipped with per pixel wide bandpass filters; and processing spectral images acquired by the imager to assess middle ear content or condition. 19. The method according to claim 18, wherein said filters comprise RGB filters. 20. The method according to claim 18, wherein said processing comprises comparing wavelengths of the light arriving from the tympanic membrane to expected wavelength ranges. 21. The method according to claim 20, wherein said comparing comprises comparing to a known set of wavelength values which define a pathogen fingerprint. 22. The method according to claim 18, wherein said processing comprises generating 2D images of the tympanic membrane from said spectral images. 23. The method according to claim 22, wherein each pixel of said 2D image is generated as a combination of K*N spectral images, N being the number of independent light sources from which the light is emitted and K being the number of different wide bandpass filters used. 24. The method according to claim 18, wherein said processing comprises determining one or more of: a transparency level of the tympanic membrane, a color of the tympanic membrane. 25. The method according to claim 18, wherein said processing comprises detecting one or both of a fluid level and a viscosity of the fluid behind the tympanic membrane. 26. The method according to claim 18, comprising diagnosing at least one of: acute otitis media (AOM) and otitis media with effusion (OME) according to said pathogen fingerprint. 27. The method according to claim 18, comprising dividing the tympanic membrane into separately illuminated segments and using a corresponding number of light sources to illuminate the segments. 28. A method for identifying pathogens in the middle ear in real time, comprising: illuminating the tympanic membrane using multiple single wavelength light sources; capturing light arriving from the tympanic membrane using an imager comprising per-pixel RGB filters; determining presence of one or more pathogens in at least one of middle ear fluid behind the tympanic membrane and the tympanic membrane itself as a function of the light detected by said RGB filters. 29. The method according to claim 28, wherein said illuminating comprises illuminating at wavelengths which are not detectable by said filters. 30. The method according to claim 28, wherein said determining comprises comparing a set of wavelengths detected by said filters to a known set of wavelengths that characterizes a pathogen. 31. The method according to claim 28, wherein said determining comprises comparing to a known fluorescence spectra of enzymes and/or coenzymes and/or amino acids of one or more of: H Influenza, M Cataralis, S Pneumoniae, S Aureas. 32. The method according to claim 28, further comprising indicating if a tympanic membrane condition was caused or effected by bacterial growth. 33. The method according to claim 28, further comprising displaying an image of the tympanic membrane captured by said imager and marking a pathogen distribution on said image. 34. The method according to claim 33, wherein said pathogen distribution includes a location of one or more pathogen colonies. 35. The method according to claim 28, wherein only light characterized by wavelengths which are detectable by at least one of said RGB filters 36. A method for identifying pathogens in the middle ear in real time, comprising: illuminating the tympanic membrane using multiple single wavelength light sources; capturing light arriving from the tympanic membrane using an imager comprising per-pixel RGB filters; wherein if light arriving from the tympanic membrane is detected by at least one of said RGB filters, pathogen presence is indicated. 37. The method according to claim 36, wherein light arriving from the tympanic membrane is detected due to presence of one or more of the following amino acids: Tryptophan, Tyrosine and Phenyloalanine which are characterized by emittance at wavelengths within wavelength ranges detectable by at least one of said RGB filters. 38. The method according to claim 37, wherein presence of Tryptophan alone is detected and is indicative of pathogen presence. 39. A device for assessing middle ear effusion, comprising: a body tapering towards a narrow end having a diameter small enough to be introduced into an external ear canal; at least two RF coils spaced apart from each other; and circuitry configured to pass an electrical current through said RF coils, measure at least one property of each of the coils, and generate an output based on a difference in the measured property between the two coils. 40. The device according to claim 39, wherein said at least one property includes: a voltage that develops across the coil, an electromagnetic field generated by the coil, an impedance of the coil. 41. The device according to claim 39, wherein said output comprises indicating presence of middle ear fluid based on said difference in the measured at least one property. 42. The device according to claim 39, wherein a diamagnetic insert is positioned in between said at least two RF coils along a similar long axis. 43. A method for assessing presence of middle ear fluid, comprising: introducing a probe comprising at least two electrically conductive elements into the external ear canal; conducting current through said elements; measuring one or more properties selected from: voltage across each of said elements, an electromagnetic field produced by each of said elements; and determining a difference in said one or more properties as measured for each of said elements to determine presence of middle ear fluid. 44. The method according to claim 43, further comprising, prior to said introducing, conducting a calibration measurement in which said probe is not in proximity to fluid, and then comparing an electromagnetic field measured inside said external ear canal to the electromagnetic field measured during said calibration measurement. 45. The method according to claim 43, wherein said introducing comprises positioning a distal end of said probe at a distance of less than 4 mm from the tympanic membrane. 46. The method according to claim 43, wherein said electrically conductive elements comprise coils and wherein said introducing comprises positioning said coils such that one is axially closer to the tympanic membrane than the other. 47. A method for assessing presence of middle ear fluid, comprising: introducing a probe comprising a ultrasound transducer into the external ear canal; excitating said ultrasound transducer to emit ultrasound signals; receiving echoes of said ultrasound signals; and analyzing said echoes to determine a change in echo signal which is indicative of presence of fluid. 48. The method according to claim 47, wherein said ultrasound transducer comprises a piezoelectric transducer, said emitting and said receiving are performed using the same piezoelectric transducer. 49. The method according to claim 47, wherein said introducing comprises positioning said probe such that said ultrasound transducer is in contact with the tympanic membrane or in contact with a liquid or gel medium contacting the tympanic membrane. 50. A device for assessing middle ear content or condition, comprising: a body extending between proximal and distal ends, the body sized for insertion, at least in part, into an external ear canal; a plurality of light sources configured at said proximal end of said body, said plurality of light sources facing distally to emit light towards the tympanic membrane when the device is inserted into the external ear canal; a distally facing imager equipped with one or more light filters; and a radio-frequency assembly incorporated within said body and including at least two electrically conductive elements spaced apart from each other.
